subject: Statistics
title: Off-Label Prescribing
description: A quick win: understand Off-Label Prescribing.

hook.text: One in five prescriptions is for a use the FDA never approved. Is that evidence-based or guesswork?

buildup.text: Off-label means using an approved drug for a different condition, age group, or dose than originally tested.

discovery.text: Some off-label uses are well-supported by studies. Aspirin for heart attacks was off-label for decades.

twist.text: But many off-label uses have no evidence at all. Doctors prescribe based on hunch, tradition, or habit.

climax.text: Off-label use exists in a statistical gray zone — sometimes life-saving, sometimes an uncontrolled experiment.

punchline.text: Approved for one thing, used for another. Evidence varies wildly.

quiz.question: What is off-label prescribing?
options: Using an approved drug for a purpose, dose, or population it wasn't originally tested for
Prescribing a drug that hasn't passed any clinical trials
Using two drugs at the same time
